description: Clodsire is an alternative bulky tank partner for Aegislash that also covers the Volt Switch Forretress matchup. Creator explicitly names Clodsire alongside Umbreon as ideal Aegislash partners.

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: I *FINALLY* solved Aegislash! 170 ELO Climb in the Great League! | Pokémon Go Battle League

description: Diggersby covers Aegislash's ghost weakness via Normal typing and fire weakness via Ground moves (Scorching Sands, Fire Punch), enabling Aegislash to safely close games in zero-shield endgames.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: XL Aegislash just hit RANK 1 world wide and its BROKEN in the Great League!

description: Umbreon's massive bulk handles Volt Switch Forretress, which is Aegislash's biggest problem. Umbreon also serves as one of the strongest Aegislash counters in the meta, surviving two blade form Gyro Balls, making it a natural complement that covers the same threats opponents would use against Aegislash.

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: I *FINALLY* solved Aegislash! 170 ELO Climb in the Great League! | Pokémon Go Battle League

description: Annihilape + Jellicent form the backline core behind a Charjabug lead. Together they make the team an 'absolute Empoleon destroyer' — the creator notes the whole team is very strong into Empoleon (the most common steel type in the meta), and when Gliscor breaks the lead, Jellicent is the fallback that covers for Annihilape's weaknesses. The creator references 'core breaker' when facing Gliscor, implying this back pair is his core.

sources.author: CalebPeng
sources.videoTitle: How to use Annihilape like a PRO!!! (Great League)

description: One of the most common cores in the current meta. Lickilicky counters flying types that threaten Annihilape, while providing ghost coverage with Shadow Ball that complements Annihilape's fighting/ghost typing.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST META TEAMS to CLIMB for the Great League in Pokemon GO Season 26

description: ABB ghost backline — both are ghost types that pressure opponents weak to ghost. Annihilape doubles as a fighter with Counter to handle normals and darks that wall Jellicent, while Jellicent's Surf covers grounds that threaten Annihilape.

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: Don't Forget... You NEED to Respect Wigglytuff in Great League!!

description: Creator calls this core 'next to perfect' — Azumarill covers Bronzong's biggest weakness to dark types, while Bronzong's steel/psychic typing and Water coverage complement Azumarill's fairy/water profile.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: BRONZONG BREAKS the current META! Destroying ANNIHILAPE and WIGGLYTUFF in the Great League

description: Azumarill and Cinderace form a Fire + Water/Fairy core. Cinderace is a glass cannon with strong Pyro Ball pressure, while Azumarill provides bulk and coverage. The creator explicitly states he already had 'the core of Azumarill as well as the Cinderace' and built the team around this pairing.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: Burning down the Great League! *NEW* PYRO BALL CINDERACE is WAY BETTER than I thought!

description: One of the most broken cores in recent seasons. Drapion got buffed significantly this season. Azumarill's water type baits out fire types which Drapion can punish with Aqua Tail for super effective damage. Their combined typings cover each other's weaknesses effectively.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST META TEAMS to CLIMB for the Great League in Pokemon GO Season 26

description: Creator explicitly names this as his core from interlude season. Charjabug's Electric/Bug typing covers Blastoise's Grass and Electric weaknesses, while Blastoise resists Fire which threatens Charjabug. Together they provide spammy neutral coverage with Discharge/X-Scissor and Hydro Cannon.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: I LOVE THIS SUBMITTED TEAM | Great League Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Corviknight can switch in to reset attack debuffs (e.g. from Chilling Water) back to zero, restoring Clodsire's Sludge Bomb to full power. Creator explicitly highlights this mechanic: 'Corvanite will be able to switch in, reset the minus two attack drop back to zero. And then Sludge Bomb will be lethal.'

sources.videoTitle: THEY MESSED UP! 🤦‍♂️ Kyurem terrorizes the Great League for the very first time | Pokémon GO PvP

description: Corviknight and Guzzlord form a defensive core where Corviknight hard walls grass types like Dartrix that threaten the team, while Guzzlord handles other threats. The creator explicitly refers to this as 'my Corviknight Guzzlord core' when explaining his strategy of switching Politoed in early to preserve this pairing for the back.

sources.author: ItsAXN
sources.videoTitle: I CAN'T Believe NO ONE Is Using Politoed [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Ground + Flying core where Corviknight replaced Sableye from the old Sableye-Swampert core. Corviknight resists grass moves that threaten Swampert, while Swampert covers Rock/Steel types. Sand Attack on both provides additional ground coverage. Got stronger this season with Swampert buffs.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST META TEAMS to CLIMB for the Great League in Pokemon GO Season 26

description: Talonflame covers Cresselia's biggest weakness — steel types that resist all of Cresselia's moves. Cresselia in turn handles fighters and provides Grass Knot coverage against ground/water types, while its Moon Blast hits dark types that might threaten the team.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: This is the *BEST* LEGENDARY for the Great League! *BUFFED* CRESSELIA is BACK being META!

description: Forretress covers the flying-type weakness that Diggersby struggles with (Gligar, Togekiss), while Diggersby's ground typing and Return handle threats Forretress can't. Creator explicitly notes Forretress is on the team to counter flying types that are bad for Diggersby.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: SURPRISING KOs! RETURN Diggersby got BUFFED this Season for the Great League

description: Creator's favorite core. Doublade's Steel/Ghost typing with Shadow Claw + Sacred Sword pairs with Brick Break Furret which handles dark types that threaten Doublade. Furret's normal typing gives it ghost immunity, complementing Doublade's ghost resistance.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST META TEAMS to CLIMB for the Great League in Pokemon GO Season 26

description: Galarian Weezing's Poison/Fairy typing combined with Drapion's Poison/Dark typing creates strong synergy. Drapion's Aqua Tail coverage handles difficult matchups for the core. Creator highlights Drapion as having received one of the biggest indirect buffs this season.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST META TEAMS to CLIMB for the Great League in Pokemon GO Season 26

description: Near-perfect core that has been dominant for 2-3 seasons. Sableye's Dark/Ghost typing and Empoleon's Water/Steel typing provide excellent mutual coverage with very few shared weaknesses. Unchanged by recent balance patches.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST META TEAMS to CLIMB for the Great League in Pokemon GO Season 26

description: Empoleon's Steel typing covers Shelgon's critical Fairy weakness, while both threaten different parts of the meta. Creator explicitly built the team around covering the dragon's fairy vulnerability with Empoleon, which also doubles as a flexible safe swap.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: My BEST BALANCED Team  | Great League Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Meta backline core that covers Ninetales' key weaknesses — Jellicent handles ground and provides bulky water coverage, while Ferrothorn covers water and rock. Creator calls them 'pretty meta team in the back' and says they are 'needed for Ninetales'.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: KANTO NINETALES RETURNS | Great League Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Ferrothorn covers Medicham's critical Ghost weakness, particularly Jellicent which walls both Ice Punch and Dynamic Punch. Creator states 'if you're going to use Medicham, use something to deal with Jellicent and all the other ghosts' — Ferrothorn fills that role.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: Best GREAT LEAGUE TEAMS | TOP RANKED POKEMON | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: ABB-style backline duo — both Dragon/Ground types that share similar typings and weaknesses, forcing the opponent to commit their counter early. Shadow Garchomp's Sand Tomb debuffs enable Gabite's Twisters to clean up, as demonstrated taking down an Altaria together ('the brothers working together').

sources.videoTitle: 20 BATTLES OF GABITE GREATNESS!! | Pokémon GO PvP

description: Creator implicitly identifies these two as a core by calling Azumarill a 'core breaker to these two Pokémon.' Heracross with Fury Cutter/Upper Hand/Rock Tomb provides fighting and rock coverage while Golisopod offers water coverage with Aqua Jet, and both share bug typing for mutual fast-attack synergy.

description: Jellicent is weak to ghost and dark; Greedent as a normal type resists ghost and can deal with those threats. Two bulky Pokémon that cover each other's weaknesses and enable the glassy Flygon closer to come in with energy/shield advantage.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

description: Double water backline that covers Alolan Ninetales' steel, fire, and rock weaknesses. Quagsire handles steels and fires with Ground/Water coverage while Jellicent adds Ghost typing for additional defensive versatility.

sources.author: ItsAXN
sources.videoTitle: ALMOST EXPERT RANK! Alolan Ninetales Is INSANE [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Near-perfect core. Wigglytuff as a fairy type handles dark and ghost types that threaten Jellicent, resists Charm damage from fairies. Jellicent handles poison types and grass types with Ice Beam that threaten Wigglytuff. Both currently dominating competitive play.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST META TEAMS to CLIMB for the Great League in Pokemon GO Season 26

description: Explicitly called 'the core' by the creator — Swampert covers Mandibuzz's weaknesses with Sludge Wave handling fairy types (especially Wigglytuff), while Mandibuzz provides flying-type pressure with Air Slash against fighters and bulk to absorb hits. Creator states this core 'is fully back now' after Mandibuzz's Foul Play and Shadow Ball buffs.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: Over 130 MMR in 3 Sets! The EASIEST team to Climb uses a forgotten Meta Monster in the Great League

description: Quagsire baits out flying-type Pokémon that threaten Poliwrath and handles them with Stone Edge, while Poliwrath provides Ice (Icy Wind) and Fighting (Dynamic Punch) coverage to dismantle the Empoleon+Altaria core that Quagsire struggles with.

description: Togekiss handles grass and dragon threats that trouble Quagsire, while Quagsire covers steel, electric, and ice weaknesses of Togekiss. Creator highlights this as a balanced pairing where you can play out the lead in most situations with only grass as a shared concern.

description: Standard Fire/Water core — Swampert handles Electric and Ice threats that trouble Talonflame, while Talonflame covers Grass weakness. Creator explicitly calls this 'a very standard core' with the safe swap built around it.
